spec_match.rationale: Scrubs currentTime through a paused animation with configurable iterations/delay/direction/duration and reports overallProgress as a normalised 0..1 against per-iteration progress — exactly the spec definition.

interactive.rationale: Four number inputs + a direction select + a range scrubber drive the animation; per-iteration bars and a big overallProgress readout update on every scrub frame.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a scrub + per-loop bar visual; the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles unsupported browsers; doesn't show direction: 'reverse' visually meaningfully (the linear translateX looks identical to 'normal' on a single axis), and the 'alternate' direction quirk where currentTime increases monotonically while visible position oscillates is not called out.

references.rationale: see-also links to the spec but NOT to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5083257285378048 directly — violates CLAUDE.md invariant 3 (every page must include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link as the self-heal anchor).

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, no raw hex, the warn block uses --accent-rose appropriately, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The page is missing the chromestatus.com/feature/5083257285378048 link in see-also. CLAUDE.md invariant 3 makes this non-negotiable — the cleanup script /tmp/fix-slugs.py uses it to recover from folder-name corruption. Add a list item linking the ChromeStatus entry.

openQuestions.title: Make direction visually meaningful
openQuestions.detail: Changing direction (normal/reverse/alternate/alternate-reverse) currently looks the same on a single horizontal axis. Either use a path that's directional (e.g. arrow shape), or add a small annotation under the ball that reads 'iteration 2 of 4 / direction alternate / playing left→right' so the effect of the controls is visible.

openQuestions.title: Show the alternate direction current-iteration paradox
openQuestions.detail: With direction: alternate, currentIteration only increments while overallProgress climbs monotonically. The per-iteration bars currently colour up left-to-right regardless of direction — surface that overallProgress is direction-agnostic by annotating the meter when direction is alternate.

summary: Conceptually strong scrubber that nails the iteration/normalisation story. Two fixes are needed: add the missing chromestatus reference link (a hard rule) and make the direction control's effect visible — otherwise three of the four configurable axes are doing invisible work.
